commits.body:
We searched the seas of Git and found,
No database keeps repos sound.
Bare files on NVMe, tried and true—
A SpinServer in Dallas will do!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Debian or Ubuntu, which to choose?
With apt on both, you cannot lose.
But Ubuntu's docs are vast and clear,
Twenty-four-oh-four LTS is here—
Five years of support, no excuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five hosts in Dallas, side by side,
NVMe and RAM, the specs compared wide.
Spin gives the most bang for the buck,
OVH plays it safe—pick your luck.
Two finalists stand, the rest step aside.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The database dream was tempting, true,
But filesystems do what databases can't do.
OVH in Dallas, NVMe and bare,
Unmetered bandwidth beyond compare—
One box to host them all, brand new.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Eight agents scoured the web in flight,
From go-git's Storer to Gitgres' might.
Four approaches found, none yet in prod—
But the wire protocol gives a nod:
Storage is yours to get right.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Eight topics to search, eight trails to blaze,
Through go-git, gitoxide, and GitHub's maze.
Can a database hold what filesystems keep?
Time to Google before we leap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Can Git live inside a database?
Can Chromium fit in that space?
Eleven leads to chase,
Seven questions to face—
Time to research the self-hosted case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three experiments, all passed with grace,
Icons, pipeline, branding in place.
The surfer rides on, the favicon gleams,
TermSurf is the name in the copyright dreams.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Identellica, we bid thee well,
Your LLC has served its spell.
TermSurf now stands in every line,
Copyright 2026—the name is mine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Identellica, your time has passed,
TermSurf's the name that's built to last.
Four files to change, one left alone—
The old issue keeps its historical tone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Sharp resizes, png-to-ico converts,
Types generated, no more old consorts.
Drop a PNG in raw-icons' embrace,
Out come assets for every place—
Retina-crisp at 3x, of course.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Raw icons in, sharp icons out,
Type-safe paths to leave no doubt.
A pipeline born from PNG,
For Retina at 3x, crisp and free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 744: new logo and issue doc
commits.body:
A surfer rides a neon wave,
The old icon sent to its grave.
New logo in place,
A fresh hooded face—
Favicon's next up to be saved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Cmd+R once reloaded config,
The browser page left hanging stiff.
Now the key flows free,
To Chromium's decree—
Refresh works. The fix? One diff.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Cmd+R, set your keys to none,
Let the browser have its fun.
Config watches files on disk—
One less shortcut, zero risk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Cmd+R pressed, config reloads—
But the browser wants its page!
The key was caught, the wrong path chose,
Now documented for the next stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Four experiments came and went,
Each one on fixing bent.
Ghost archived, webtui installed,
Sudo tamed, uninstall recalled—
742 is done. We rest content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Webtui was left out of "all,"
And sudo was missing its call.
One gate at the top,
Three bugs made to stop—
Now uninstall truly removes all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Uninstall script forgot its webtui,
And sudo it lacked, oh me oh my.
A plan is now laid—
Three fixes are made,
One gate, one call, one last goodbye.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 742: single sudo for install script
commits.body:
Thirteen sudos walked into a bar,
Each one demanding a password by far.
"Exec once at the top!"
Now the prompts finally stop—
One gate to rule them, au revoir.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Thirteen sudos walked into a bar,
The bouncer said, "You've gone too far."
Now one sudo guards the door,
And the rest need type no more.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A stub once stood where code should be,
"cargo install" it said, unhelpfully.
Now the script copies the binary right,
To /usr/local/bin in broad daylight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The web TUI had no place to stay,
install.sh just shrugged and walked away.
Experiment 2 draws up the plan:
sudo cp, like a moving van.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The archive worked, the docs are clean,
But webtui install? Nowhere to be seen.
It prints a message, helpful and polite—
But /usr/local/bin stays empty tonight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Ghostboard served us well, a Zig-built shrine,
But two GUIs for one protocol's a sign
Of doubled work with every proto change—
So Wezboard stands alone upon the range.

The code sleeps safe in git's eternal keep,
While docs preserve the architecture deep.
When protocols are stable, calm, and clear,
A fresh fork of Ghostty shall appear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
One experiment to rule them all:
Delete the dir, update each call,
Scripts trimmed, docs moved—
The archive approved—
Ghostboard sleeps, but will stand tall.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Dear Ghostboard, we must part ways—
Not forever, just for a phase.
Wezboard carries the torch alone,
Till the protocol is fully grown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The GUI was a middleman, passing notes in class—
Now TUI and browser talk direct, no more through the glass.
Four experiments later, the protocol stands free,
Seven-forty-one is done. On to seven-forty-three!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 741: shorten socket path, pass experiment 4
commits.body:
The socket path grew long and proud,
Too many bytes—the kernel howled.
A hash, a name, a trim, a snip—
Now Unix lets the bytes all fit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A socket path grew long and wide,
Past SUN_LEN it could not slide.
Now name plus hash, compact and neat,
Keeps the bind from tasting defeat.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The forwarding paths were cut,
Direct lines were strung—
Pages loaded just fine, but
Dark mode held its tongue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five proxies walked through Wezboard's door,
Relaying messages—what a bore.
Experiment 3 says "you're fired,"
Your forwarding's no longer required,
The TUI talks direct evermore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The browser and TUI now speak directly—
no middleman required for Navigate,
SetColorScheme, or content events.
The GUI just whispers "BrowserReady"
and steps politely out of the way.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The TUI whispered to the GUI,
"Why must you stand between us, why?"
Now BrowserReady shows the way—
Direct socket, no relay.
The middleman can only sigh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The code was right, the tests were tight,
But no one passed the flag at night.
Wezboard spawns Roamium bare—
No --listen-socket anywhere.
Can't test a door that's never there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 741: add listener socket to Roamium
commits.body:
A lone socket stood in the dark,
Speaking only to one GUI spark.
Now it listens wide,
Lets clients inside,
And broadcasts to all in the park.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A fork deserves its credit due,
Wez Longboard, we acknowledge you.
MIT in full, directory set right—
Ghostboard's path corrected tonight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 741: simplify to broadcast, drop TuiRegister
commits.body:
No registration needed, no routing games—
The browser just shouts to all its frames.
Connect and listen, that's all you do,
Every event flows straight to you.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 741: stage Wezboard first, Ghostboard last
commits.body:
Prove it once in Rust, then port to Zig—
Four experiments, small to big.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A document grown through debate and revision,
Rewritten with clarity, structure, precision.
Same destination, a cleaner map—
No vestigial detours, no circular trap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The GUI relayed what it never read,
A middleman with nothing to be said.
Now each process owns its role alone—
Window, content, chrome — three to the bone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three protos seemed right, then we thought twice—
One browser API for every device.
GUI or TUI, the browser won't care,
Just register your type and pull up a chair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
"Why build a bridge to tear it down?"
The proxy detour earned a frown.
Three pieces, staged, no throwaway—
Direct sockets win the day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The hub grew tired of passing notes,
Between the TUI and browser boats.
"Why not talk direct?" they said one day—
Three channels now, each its own way.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 740: record pass and close issue
commits.body:
Two experiments, both alike in dignity—
One fixed the menu, one the dock.
Now "TermSurf Wezboard" reigns with equanimity,
Across every UI block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 740 Exp 2: rename app bundle directory
commits.body:
The dock said "Wezboard" — how plain!
The bundle's old name was to blame.
We renamed the .app,
Updated each path and map,
Now "TermSurf Wezboard" stakes its claim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The menu bar learned its brand new name,
But the dock icon stayed the same.
Info.plist tried its best—
macOS said, "I'm not impressed,
The bundle path is what I claim."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dock said "Wezboard," plain and bare,
No family name to show it cared.
"TermSurf Wezboard" — now that's a name
That tells the world from whence it came.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 739: record pass and close issue
commits.body:
Five warnings haunted the build,
The compiler grumbled, unfulfilled.
Two experiments later—all clear,
Not a single warning to hear.
The release log? Peacefully stilled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 739 Exp 2: fix four Wezboard warnings
commits.body:
Four warnings walked into a build,
The compiler scowled, not thrilled.
Dead code got the axe,
Unused vars got underscore tax—
Now release builds are warning-free guild.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
